quoteFirst: I pressed her hand and spoke to     her; but she neither saw me, heard me, nor seemed in the least     sensible; and in this state she lay for five hours, namely, till
quoteLast: , when she departed, no one being     present but myself, M. Haine, a good friend of ours whom my father     knows, and the nurse.
title: Blackwood's Edinburgh Magazine, Volume 62, Number 361, November, 1845.
